response.justification: Wei Chen is a full-stack web and embedded software developer with a career focused on TypeScript, Node.js, web platforms, and GIS tooling. The Principal Performance Architect role requires deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator workloads, hardware-software co-design, and GPU/SIMD architecture — none of which appear anywhere in this resume. The candidate's closest hardware-adjacent experience is C/C++ component work for a C IDE at National Instruments and low-latency async I/O at Dunnhumby, both of which are far removed from the specialized silicon and deep-learning performance analysis this role demands. There is no signal of AI/ML workload expertise, no architecture design at the chip or system level, and no published or project work in the relevant domain.
strengths: 14+ years of continuous software engineering experience, satisfying the Bachelor's + 8 years experience threshold on paper
Demonstrated low-latency systems work (async I/O, fibers, high-performance databases) at Dunnhumby shows some performance-oriented mindset
C and C++ experience at National Instruments provides a thin connection to lower-level, compiled-language development
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, hardware simulation, or chip-level architecture — the core technical requirement of the role
No background in AI/ML frameworks, deep neural network workloads, GPU architecture, or any accelerator-related performance analysis
Role demands Principal/Senior IC hardware expertise; candidate's entire career arc is web, mobile, and application-layer software

key_factors.factor: No AI/DL or GPU workload expertise
Web/application software background vs. hardware architecture
